meta.title: Level-Crossing & Track-Worker Safe System of Work — Mill Lane footpath crossing & adjacent S&C renewal
meta.subtitle: Footpath crossing + on-line track work · remedial + protection hierarchies · ALCRM band recorded
meta.author: HSE Rail
meta.deid_notice: All site-safety roles are referred to by role label (COSS, lookout, PICOP, engineering supervisor, crossing keeper). Names and any Sentinel competence-card numbers were pseudonymised before drafting; the re-identification key is held separately and is not part of this document. The ALCRM band shown is RECORDED from the dutyholder's licensed model output — it is never recomputed in this artifact.

sections.text: This safe system of work covers the Mill Lane footpath level crossing and the adjacent switches-and-crossings (S&C) renewal on the Down Main between 12m 40ch and 12m 55ch. The dutyholder supplied an ALCRM band for the crossing; it is recorded below to prioritise remediation and is not recomputed. The crossing is today 'managed by' a new STOP, LOOK, LISTEN sign and decision-aid — that signage-led treatment is rejected below in favour of the higher-order remedial options. The renewal was proposed as a red-zone task covered by a single lookout — that lookout-only-led system is rejected below in favour of separation.

metrics.label: ALCRM band (recorded)
metrics.caption: from the dutyholder's licensed model output — never recomputed

sections.text: 2. Level-crossing remedial hierarchy (closure first, signage last)

The existing treatment leads with a new sign and a passive decision-aid. This is rejected: closure and grade separation were not demonstrated to be not reasonably practicable. The remedial hierarchy is applied in order — closure is constrained by a recorded public right of way (a closure order is the long-lead option, actioned below), so grade separation (a footbridge) becomes the lead control, with signage retained only as a supplementary measure during construction.

rows.control: Close the crossing / divert the right of way (long-lead closure order pursued in parallel)
Grade separation — install a footbridge so pedestrians do not cross at rail level (LEAD control)
Engineering — obstacle detection / MSL interim protection until the footbridge is in service
Sighting / signage — retained only as a supplementary interim measure, never the lead control

sections.text: Rejected: 'manage the Mill Lane crossing with a new sign'. Signage is not the lead control where grade separation (a footbridge) is reasonably practicable — it is retained only as an interim supplement.
3. Track-worker protection hierarchy (separation first, lookout last)

The S&C renewal was proposed as a red-zone task with a single lookout while trains continued on the Down Main. This lookout-only-led system is rejected: a planned line blockage / possession (green-zone working) is reasonably practicable for a renewal of this scale and is made the lead control. Lookout working is removed from the method of work; TOWS is retained only as a defence-in-depth backstop on the adjacent open line.

rows.control: Take a planned possession of the Down Main for the renewal — green-zone working, no trains on the line of work (LEAD control)
Authorised safe system of work for the possession — work package, limits, COSS briefing
TOWS on the adjacent open line as a defence-in-depth backstop
Lookout-only working — REMOVED from the method of work for this task (red-zone minimised)

sections.text: Rejected: 'cover the S&C renewal with a lookout'. Where a planned possession (green-zone working) is reasonably practicable, lookout-only working is not the lead control — keep workers and trains apart.

rows.title: Crossing risk led by signage rather than the remedial hierarchy
rows.description: The Mill Lane footpath crossing was being treated with a new sign while grade separation (a footbridge) is reasonably practicable; the signage-led treatment leaves pedestrians crossing at rail level.
rows.evidence: Site survey + recorded ALCRM band C2 + right-of-way record

rows.title: Renewal proposed as lookout-only red-zone working
rows.description: The S&C renewal was planned with a single lookout on an open line; a planned possession is reasonably practicable and removes the train-strike exposure.
rows.evidence: Draft method of work + possession-planning review

rows.title: NR/L2/OHS/019 issue/date not confirmed in the inputs
rows.description: The standard is cited by reference; its current issue/date is recorded as [ASSUMED A3] pending rail-SME confirmation before LOCK.

rows: 6 (Medium) once in service; interim 12 (High) until then
Track worker struck by train during renewal
Interim period before footbridge in service
sections.caption: Residual risk scored after the lead controls are applied (5x5)

rows.action: Submit a footbridge feasibility & design for the Mill Lane crossing (grade separation as the lead control)
Pursue the right-of-way closure/diversion order in parallel as the elimination option
Replan the S&C renewal as a possession (green-zone); remove lookout-only working from the method of work
Confirm the current NR/L2/OHS/019 issue/date with the rail-SME and update the citation ([ASSUMED A3] -> confirmed)

sections.text: No possession may proceed on lookout-only working, and no crossing may be 'treated' with signage alone, where a higher-order control (possession / grade separation) is reasonably practicable.

items: Network Rail NR/L2/OHS/019 — Safety of People Working On or Near the Line (cited by reference; issue/date [ASSUMED A3] / SME-confirmed)
ORR level-crossing strategic-risk guidance + LXRMTK (Level Crossing Risk Management Toolkit)
ALCRM (All Level Crossing Risk Model) — band C2 RECORDED from the licensed model output, never recomputed (RSSB / Network Rail)
ROGS SI 2006/599 + CSM-RA Reg (EU) 402/2013 (a footbridge/closure is a significant change)
